reasoning: All five message types required by RFC 8446 Section 5.1 to be aligned with a record boundary have the corresponding enforcement check implemented via RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending. (1) ServerHello: tls_process_server_hello (statem_clnt.c:1967-1973) checks for TLS 1.3 with RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending and calls SSLfatal with SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE if not on record boundary. (2) Finished: tls_process_finished (statem_lib.c:855-860) does the same check for TLS 1.3. (3) KeyUpdate: tls_process_key_update (statem_lib.c:706-711) does an unconditional check since KeyUpdate is TLS 1.3 only. (4) EndOfEarlyData: tls_process_end_of_early_data (statem_srvr.c:4735-4740) checks RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending and fatals. (5) ClientHello: tls_early_post_process_client_hello (statem_srvr.c:1902-1907) checks for TLS 1.3 and fatals if not on record boundary. All five required messages have the record boundary check enforced on receive. The check works by detecting whether there is any processed data remaining in the current record after the message was parsed; if so, the message did not end at a record boundary, which is treated as a fatal protocol violation with unexpected_message alert.

evidence.expected_behavior: ClientHello, EndOfEarlyData, ServerHello, Finished, and KeyUpdate must each be sent aligned with a record boundary. On receive, if extra data exists in the same record after any of these messages, the implementation must reject it with an unexpected_message alert.
